e_visa.nationality: All nationalities (ordinary passport holders not in visa-free category)
e_visa.notes: E-visa (pre-enrollment online + biometric enrollment at Abidjan airport) available through snedai.com, the only officially state-recognized and accredited site for Côte d'Ivoire visa applications. Valid 3 months (multiple entries). Cost: 73 Euros including bank fees. Processing: up to 48 working hours (Monday–Friday). Upon arrival at Félix Houphouët-Boigny International Airport (Abidjan), applicant presents pre-enrollment approval document and completes biometric enrollment at 'Visa aéroport' (open 24/7). Ordinary passports only, minimum 6 months validity. Yellow fever vaccination certificate required at airport. Required documents: passport copy, return air ticket, invitation letter or accommodation certificate (must be typed, printed, signed, stamped, and legalized). The CEPICI official PDF (2018) confirms the e-visa process and 73 EUR cost.

visa_policy.notes: Côte d'Ivoire requires a visa for most nationalities. The official e-visa portal is snedai.com (officially recognized and accredited by the State of Côte d'Ivoire). Embassy biometric visa costs 58 Euros total (50 Euros dossier + 5 Euros RDV appointment + 3 Euros online processing fee). Holders of diplomatic or service passports are exempt from the visa fee but must still apply. The CEPICI official PDF (October 2018, sftp.225invest.ci) provides the authoritative text-format enumeration of visa-free countries. Rwanda was added as visa-free in June 2019.

conditional_access.conditions: France–Côte d'Ivoire Agreement on reciprocal short-stay visa exemption for diplomatic passport holders, signed in Paris on 20 January 2025, in force from 1 June 2025 (Décret n° 2025-357 du 18 avril 2025, published on legifrance.gouv.fr). Diplomatic passport must retain at least 3 months validity beyond intended departure date. Must be accompanied by official diplomatic note or mission order. No employment (including self-employment) permitted. Multiple stays not exceeding 90 days within any 180-day period. Covers France (metropolitan departments, overseas regions/departments, overseas collectivities, New Caledonia) and Côte d'Ivoire territory. Diplomatic passports only — service and official passports are NOT covered by this specific agreement.
conditional_access.notes: Applies to holders of French diplomatic passports entering Côte d'Ivoire and to holders of Ivorian diplomatic passports entering France. Diplomatic passports only — service and official passports are NOT covered by this specific agreement.

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of diplomatic or service category passports issued to nationals of Austria, Brazil, China, Gabon, Israel, South Africa, Turkey, Uganda, Venezuela and Vietnam do not require a visa for Côte d'Ivoire. Source: Côte d'Ivoire Ministry of Foreign Affairs official document dated 15 March 2013 (Conseil aux voyageurs / Annexe.pdf). Note: The CEPICI PDF (2018) lists these same countries with 'passeport ordinaire' notation for some (e.g., South Africa, Gabon, Israel, Uganda, Brazil) in the 'visa required but no prior DST consultation' list — this indicates their ORDINARY passports require a visa, while diplomatic/service passports are exempt as per the 2013 MFA Annexe document. No stay duration specified in source.
conditional_access.notes: Original source is an official Côte d'Ivoire MFA document (diplomatie.gouv.ci domain), dated 15 March 2013. Document is archived (Wayback Machine). The CEPICI 2018 PDF (official government source, sftp.225invest.ci) confirms that South Africa, Brazil, Gabon, Israel, and Uganda holders of ordinary passports require a visa — consistent with their diplomatic/service passport exemption being the operative rule. Current applicability of these agreements (2024–2026) unverified against a live official source, but confirmed in both the 2013 and 2018 official documents.

conditional_access.conditions: Côte d'Ivoire and Swiss Federal Council agreement on reciprocal suppression of visas for holders of diplomatic and service passports, signed in Berne (Switzerland). Ratified by Côte d'Ivoire Council of Ministers on 9 November 2022. Covers: Ivorian and Swiss nationals holding valid diplomatic or service passports, members of diplomatic missions, consular posts or permanent missions, family members, and nationals participating in official visits, meetings or conferences organised by either party or by an organisation with which a headquarters agreement has been concluded.
conditional_access.notes: Source is KOACI (private news outlet, not an official government domain). The official AIP (Agence Ivoirienne de Presse, state press agency) article that originally reported this is no longer accessible (404). The Swiss SEM official list of diplomatic visa exemptions does not currently list Côte d'Ivoire. Recorded with source_official=false pending confirmation from an official government domain.

conditional_access.conditions: Côte d'Ivoire and Republic of the Congo agreement on visa exemption for nationals holding diplomatic or service passports, signed June 2023, ratified by Côte d'Ivoire Council of Ministers on 14 March 2024. Note: Republic of Congo (COG) ordinary passport holders already have unconditional visa-free access (see visa_policy.visa_free). This diplomatic/service entry in conditional_access is now superseded for COG in practice by the ordinary passport exemption, but the 2024 diplomatic/service agreement is recorded for completeness.
conditional_access.notes: Source is agenceafrique.com (private news outlet). No official government domain source found for this 2023/2024 agreement. Congo ordinary passports are already visa-free per the 2013 MFA document and 2018 CEPICI PDF. Recorded with source_official=false pending confirmation from an official domain.

credential.subtype: titre de séjour (any valid residence permit)

conditional_access.conditions: Holders of any valid residence permit (titre de séjour) are not required to obtain a visa to enter Côte d'Ivoire, regardless of nationality. Confirmed in the SNEDAI FAQ (official state-accredited visa portal): 'Est-il nécessaire de solliciter un visa lorsqu'on est en possession d'un titre de séjour ? Non.'
conditional_access.notes: This rule is stated in the SNEDAI FAQ without specifying which country's residence permit is accepted. It is likely intended to cover any valid foreign residence permit, but the precise scope (e.g., only Ivorian residence permit, or any foreign residence permit) is not clarified in the official text. The FAQ answer is unambiguous: 'No' — no visa required for residence permit holders.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Côte d'Ivoire government source. No official program page was found on diplomatie.gouv.ci, snedai.com, or any other official domain.

agreements: ECOWAS (Economic Community of West African States / CEDEAO) free movement of persons
UEMOA (Union Économique et Monétaire Ouest Africaine)

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Expanded visa_policy.visa_free from 1 group entry (ECOWAS only) to 24 individual country entries, using the official CEPICI PDF (sftp.225invest.ci, October 2018 — an official Côte d'Ivoire government source) which contains the full text-format list: ECOWAS members + Central African Republic, Republic of Congo (COG), Chad, Mauritania, Morocco, Philippines, Seychelles, Singapore, Tunisia. Rwanda was added June 2019 per bilan.gouv.ci (official).

CORRECTED: ECOWAS members disaggregated into individual country entries with iso3 codes to match the graph node model. The prior single group entry 'ECOWAS member state nationals' was replaced by 14 individual country entries.

CORRECTED: Republic of Congo (COG) moved to visa_policy.visa_free for ordinary passports (confirmed in CEPICI 2018 official PDF). The prior conditional_access entry for COG was for a 2023/2024 diplomatic/service passport agreement — this is now noted as superseded for ordinary passports but retained for the diplomatic-passport-specific agreement in conditional_access.
CORRECTED: Rwanda moved from 'gap' status to visa_policy.visa_free — confirmed visa-free from June 2019 based on bilan.gouv.ci (official government source) and Wikipedia citing the same.
CORRECTED: Added Morocco, Tunisia, Mauritania, Chad, Central African Republic, Philippines, Seychelles, Singapore as individual visa_free entries — all confirmed from the official CEPICI PDF (2018).
CORRECTED: Added new conditional_access entry for residence permit holders ('titre de séjour') — confirmed in SNEDAI FAQ (official state-accredited portal). Prior record did not capture this rule.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: e-visa process, cost (73 EUR), procedures — all verified against current snedai.com/e-visa/ (scraped June 2026). No change.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: France–Côte d'Ivoire diplomatic passport agreement (legifrance.gouv.fr) — verified, in force from 1 June 2025.
CONFIRMED UNCHANGED: cbi.has_program=false — no CBI program found.

NOTE: The CEPICI PDF also lists some countries (Austria, Brazil, China, Gabon, Israel, South Africa, Turkey, Uganda, Venezuela) as requiring a visa with 'passeport ordinaire' annotation — these are listed under Option 1 (visa required, no prior DST consultation). This is consistent with: their ordinary passport holders require a visa; their diplomatic/service passport holders are exempt per the 2013 MFA Annexe document.
gaps: ECOWAS member stay-duration: ECOWAS protocol provides free movement and right of establishment but no explicit maximum stay days in official text found on diplomatie.gouv.ci. Most ECOWAS sources indicate indefinite residence right rather than 90-day cap.

Scope of 'titre de séjour' residence permit exemption: SNEDAI FAQ does not specify whether any foreign residence permit is accepted or only an Ivorian residence permit (titre de séjour). Likely means an Ivorian residence permit but wording is ambiguous.

Switzerland (CHE) diplomatic/service passport visa exemption agreement: ratification announced 9 November 2022 via Côte d'Ivoire Council of Ministers (reported by KOACI news). No confirmation found on any live official government domain (diplomatie.gouv.ci, SEM Swiss official list). Recorded with source_official=false.

Haiti diplomatic/service passport exemption: Wikipedia notes Haiti (diplomatic, official, service passports) added 29 March 2013, later cancelled (date unknown). Current status unclear — not recorded pending official confirmation.
No conditional entry rule found for holders of US, Schengen, UK, Canadian, Australian, Japanese, UAE/GCC visas — confirmed absent from all official and aggregator sources.
No OCI or equivalent special diaspora/heritage status entry rule found — confirmed absent.

UEMOA and CEN-SAD member nationals beyond ECOWAS/already-listed: not explicitly confirmed as visa-free on any scraped official page. Mali, Burkina Faso, Niger, Senegal, Togo, Benin, Guinea-Bissau are ECOWAS members and already listed. Guinea, Mauritania covered separately. CEN-SAD members not in ECOWAS not verified.

Current validity of the 2013 MFA document diplomatic/service passport agreements (Austria, Brazil, China, Gabon, Israel, South Africa, Turkey, Uganda, Venezuela, Vietnam): both the 2013 MFA Annexe and 2018 CEPICI PDF are official documents but neither is a live/current web page. No 2024-2026 update found.
Fast-track, digital-nomad, startup, or skilled worker visa schemes: no official program page found — confirmed absent.

outbound_official.note: no official Côte d'Ivoire government outbound visa-list page located; rely on destination-side data
